channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Rejects US Ceasefire Proposal as "Surrender," Trump Calls Response "Totally Unacceptable"
item.description: Iran sent its response to the US peace proposal via Pakistani mediators, demanding war reparations, full sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and an end to sanctions. Trump rejected the response and accused Tehran of "playing games" for nearly 50 years.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Suggests Ukraine Conflict "Coming to an End" After Scaled-Back Victory Day Parade
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he believes the war in Ukraine is nearing its end, while condemning Western support for Kyiv. The Victory Day parade in Moscow featured no military hardware for the first time in nearly two decades amid fears of Ukrainian attacks.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon Reports 39 Killed in Israeli Strikes Despite Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: Israeli strikes in Lebanon have killed at least 39 people, including women and children, according to Lebanon's health ministry. The attacks come despite a US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, with Israel saying it targets Hezbollah infrastructure.

item.title: ⚠️ Gulf States Report Drone Attacks Straining Fragile US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: Qatar, Kuwait, and the UAE reported drone incidents as the US-Iran ceasefire faces mounting pressure. A cargo ship was struck in Qatari waters, while Iran warned that any attack on its vessels would trigger a "heavy assault" on US bases.

item.title: ⚽ Iran Demands World Cup Visas for Ex-Revolutionary Guard Team Members
item.description: Iran's soccer federation insists that players and staff who served in the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps should receive visas for the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the US, Canada, and Mexico. The team captain Mehdi Taremi completed his mandatory military service in the Guard.

item.title: 🇵🇸 West Bank Settlers Force Palestinian to Dig Up Father's Body
item.description: UN human rights office condemned the incident as "appalling and emblematic of the dehumanisation of Palestinians" in the West Bank, where settlers forced a man to exhume his father's freshly buried body.

item.title: 🚢 Hantavirus Cruise Ship Passengers Begin Flying Home
item.description: Passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship in Tenerife after a hantavirus outbreak killed three people are being repatriated to their home countries as health authorities trace contacts.

item.title: 🛢️ Saudi Aramco Profit Jumps 25% After Shifting from Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Saudi Arabia's state oil giant reported a significant Q1 profit increase after rerouting oil exports away from the Strait of Hormuz during the Iran conflict, highlighting the company's adaptability.

item.title: ⛪ Nobel Laureate Smuggled Memoir Details Iranian Prison Abuse
item.description: Narges Mohammadi, arrested 14 times for activism, smuggled writings describe beatings and neglect in Iranian prisons, offering a disturbing glimpse into conditions amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Iran War to Overshadow Trump-Xi Summit as Tensions Mount
item.description: The US-China relationship faces new strains as Trump prepares to visit China amid the Iran conflict, with trade tensions and geopolitical calculations complicating diplomatic efforts.

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvian Defense Minister Resigns After Drone Airspace Violation
item.description: Latvian Defense Minister Andris Spruds resigned after drones violated Latvian airspace, with PM Evika Silina saying he had lost her trust and that of the public.

item.title: 🇰🇷🛢️ South Korea Draws Gulf Oil Storage Interest as Hormuz Stays Closed
item.description: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait and the UAE are among major oil producers looking to South Korea underground storage facilities as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Car Bomb Kills 15 Police Officers
item.description: A suicide bomber attacked a police checkpoint in Pakistan late Saturday, killing 15 police officers. The attack highlights ongoing security challenges in the region.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Breaches Ukraine Ceasefire, Zelenskyy Says
item.description: Russia violated a three-day ceasefire with Ukraine, according to President Zelenskyy, as Putin simultaneously hints at potential peace talks.

item.title: 🇬🇧 British Paratroopers Deploy to Remote Island for Hantavirus Case
item.description: Army parachutists landed on Tristan da Cunha, Britain's most remote overseas territory, to deliver medical aid for a suspected hantavirus case linked to the cruise ship outbreak.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump Faces Diplomatic Tightrope Ahead of Xi Summit
item.description: With Tehran, Taiwan, and trade tensions looming, the US president enters talks with China from a vulnerable position while hoping for economic wins.

item.title: 🪂 British paratroopers deploy to remote island for hantavirus case
item.description: A specialist army team parachuted onto Tristan da Cunha, one of the world's most remote inhabited islands, to treat a Briton with suspected hantavirus from the affected cruise ship.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel deports Gaza flotilla activists
item.description: Spain's Saif Abu Keshek and Brazil's Thiago Ávila were deported by Israel after being detained aboard a Gaza-bound aid flotilla. The vessel was intercepted by Israeli authorities.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia's One Nation wins historic parliamentary seat
item.description: The right-wing One Nation party secured its first seat in the lower house in a by-election seen as a key test for the populist movement in Australian politics.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Labour crisis deepens as Starmer faces leadership challenge
item.description: Keir Starmer's position as UK Prime Minister appears increasingly precarious as around 40 Labour MPs call for his resignation. Health Secretary Wes Streeting and Deputy PM Angela Rayner are reportedly preparing leadership bids.

item.title: 🛸 DARPA seeks containerized drone swarm systems for autonomous operations
item.description: DARPA issued a request for concepts for containerized systems that can launch, recover and support drone swarms of up to 500 UAVs, designed for deployment behind enemy lines with minimal human involvement.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary's New PM Apologizes to Those Wronged Under Orbán
item.description: Péter Magyar, who swept away 16 years of Viktor Orbán's rule, struck a radically different tone in his first speech as Hungary's new prime minister.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Finds Military Spy Drone Near Russian Kaliningrad Border
item.description: Polish military police discovered a reconnaissance drone with Cyrillic markings near the border with Russia's Kaliningrad exclave. Officials say the drone had no combat capabilities but was likely used for intelligence gathering.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Sent Response to US War Proposal via Pakistan, State Media Reports
item.description: Iran conveyed its response to a US proposal to end the conflict through Pakistani channels, according to Iranian state media. Pakistan has served as an intermediary in diplomatic communications between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 🇨🇳 US Sanctions Nine China and Hong Kong Entities Over Iran Military Links
item.description: The US Treasury and State Department imposed sanctions on mainland China and Hong Kong entities accused of enabling Iran's military procurement efforts. The move comes days before Trump's summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing.
